_also_assesses: idea-crosslingual-phrase-coverage-io-compression (idea A; A-E2 blocked-on-inputs, priority read only — no A-E2 result exists to interpret yet)
_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record per docs/next/research-engine.md SS2.1, placed at registry/assessments/<id>.json alongside the f2b-replicate record (the SS2.1 candidates/<id>/assessment/ namespace, deltas D1/D3, is not yet built). This assessment interprets a family of Tier-0 GATING censuses (bead kernel-of-truth-5iu), NOT a frozen experiment: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md is explicitly a DESIGN/PLANNING doc ('Nothing here is pre-registered; nothing spends money; no experiment entry is created'). There is therefore NO frozen SAP and NO verdict object; the K-B0 kill margin is a STIPULATED sanity default 'reset at freeze' and no freeze happened, so no formal kill can FIRE. Backing is the honesty-guard tooling-measurement rail (committed output + reproduce command), and the conclusion below is an INTERPRETIVE PARK recommendation, not a fired verdict.
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 4 and the kern Fable/Opus execution split: Opus ran the deterministic censuses and asserted MEASURED counts only; what the counts MEAN, and whether idea-B clears K-B0, is decided here and only here. One of 4 concurrent feasibility-push agents; scope = registry/assessments ONLY (did not touch audit-status.jsonl, assumptions.jsonl, or the a5-llm audit).
_reverification: 2026-07-10 interpreter pass (bead kernel-of-truth-ain2, distinct from the census-execution bead kernel-of-truth-5iu cited in _placement_note): all four measurement_refs sha256 re-verified byte-identical against poc/compression-census/results/; every quoted number re-checked against b-e0-tinystories.md and b-rt-roundtrip.json; INTERPRETATION UNCHANGED. The four still-open assumptions below are now formally registered in registry/assumptions.jsonl as ASM-0100 (K-B0 floor, STIPULATED), ASM-0101 (second-corpus stipulation, STIPULATED), ASM-0102 (second-corpus direction, EXTRAPOLATION, load_bearing false), ASM-0103 (word-mass proxy for the prefill-token floor, STIPULATED) - previously informal ids only. Downstream consumers verified consistent with this record's envelope: docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md section 2d (engagement ~0.6%, ~5x below floor, PARKED-not-killed) and registry/assessments/g8.json cross_experiment_picture (the ~0.006 proposition-engagement point on the monotone coverage gradient 1.0 self-authored -> 0.354 m0b -> 0.006 compression -> ~0.0 Mathlib; g8's itemised reject histogram independently re-finds vocabulary as the binding constraint, 72% unmapped-constant first-fail, in the formal sector).

b_e0.corpus: TinyStories-valid, sha256 94e43181..., 21990 stories, 405523 sentences, 3786603 expanded word tokens (m0a/m0b pinned corpus)
b_e0.kernel_instance: kernel-v0 (54 concepts) + molecules-v0 (54 labels + corpusLemmas synonyms), 217 lexicon entries — the m0b molecules-v0 rung instance

a_e2_blocker.status: BLOCKED-ON-INPUTS — A-E2 was NOT run (no result to interpret); external frequency lists + host tokenizers + non-English surface data are absent locally and the Tier-0 constraint is $0/no-fetch.

_surprise_note: No prereg claim.md / registered prior exists (this is a pre-freeze gating census), so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Informally: engagement this low was the design's own live suspicion (io-compression-ideas.md SS3.6 warned engagement is 'necessarily <= conjunctive proposition-level coverage, which the linter design already warns may be far below the 0.3542 token-mass number', and called B-E0 'the idea's live-or-die number'). The result is on-thesis for that warning; the information content is captured in the park decision and questions_opened, not a surprise flag.

interpretation.headline: Idea B (compositional roll-up) does NOT clear the K-B0 engagement floor at this scope, and the two named caveats (single-favourable-corpus, conservative-v0-instrument) are NOT load-bearing enough to require a rescue pass before recording the outcome — because neither can plausibly close a ~5x gap and both point the wrong way or off-target. But this is a PARK, not a mechanism kill: B-RT proves the machinery is exact (100% round-trip), and the binding constraint is vocabulary coverage, which is precisely the coverage-growth lever the K-B0 kill text parks the idea behind. Idea A's priority is NOT lowered by B-E0 (different estimand, different corpus); the tokenizer fetch it needs is dual-use and cheap, but the frequency-corpus source-pinning + pilot-language + A-F1 decisions are unfrozen design choices — boundary-stop.
interpretation.kill_chain_read_idea_b: The K-B0 quantity is 'achievable corpus savings'. MEASURED savings fraction of corpus word mass: 0.399% (default-v0.1.0/strict) to 0.444% (a1-hybrid/permissive-det); engagement (expanded-word mass inside maximal parseable fragments) 0.592% to 0.643%. The K-B0 sanity-default floor is 2% of workload prefill tokens [STIPULATED; reset at freeze]. Savings is therefore ~4.5-5.0x below the floor and engagement ~3.1-3.4x below, on EVERY cell, including the most permissive (and LOSSY, hence non-lossless-eligible) a1-hybrid/permissive-det cell. Stratum decomposition (strict): is-a 0.243% is the dominant contributor, simple-clause 0.118%, operator-complex 0.037%, clause-AND 0.0002% (n=1 fragment in 405,523 sentences). The maintainer's flagship native pattern 'a AND b' (clause-AND) engages ~0 on real text. NO formal kill FIRES (nothing was frozen); the interpretive conclusion is PARK.
licenses: [MEASURED] B-E0 engagement/savings: on TinyStories-valid (94e43181..., 21,990 stories), the kernel-v0+molecules-v0 roll-up instrument engages 0.592-0.643% of expanded word mass and yields 0.399-0.444% net token savings — decisively (~4.5-5x) below the K-B0 2% sanity floor. WITHIN THIS SCOPE (this corpus, this kernel instance, this fail-closed v0 parser) roll-up compression is not worth its complexity: idea-B is PARKED per the K-B0 kill's own text ('idea parks behind coverage growth (idea A) and/or the OR extension, with the census as the re-entry gate').

[MEASURED] The binding constraint is VOCABULARY COVERAGE, not the roll-up grammar or parse permissiveness: the dominant abstain cause is unmapped-word = 2,068,450 (a1-hybrid/strict), an order of magnitude above the next causes (pronoun-anaphor 461,962; open-class-verb 287,439). Engagement is bounded by what the mapper/kernel can name, i.e. the SAME m0b-coverage lever (0.3542 @ molecules-v0). This is why the outcome is a PARK behind coverage growth and not a mechanism defect.

[MEASURED] Caveat 1 (single favourable corpus) does NOT rescue: TinyStories-valid is the deliberately favourable ~1.5k-lemma m0b continuity domain, i.e. the BEST-case corpus for a coverage-bounded engagement number. The park rests on the measured favourable-corpus savings (~0.4%) alone, which is already ~5x below the floor; it does not depend on any other corpus.

[MEASURED] Caveat 2 (conservative v0 instrument) does NOT rescue: the measured permissiveness delta is tiny and off-target. Relaxing strict->permissive-det moves savings only 0.399%->0.439% (~+10% relative; 0.592%->0.636% engagement), and that relaxation is a FLAGGED LOSSY determiner convention (the/that/those -> THIS) ineligible for a lossless-roll-up claim. A ~5x gap cannot be closed by a lever whose measured reach is ~10% and whose target (parse conventions) is not the binding constraint (unmapped-word vocabulary). Permissiveness passes act on parse conventions; the gap lives in vocabulary.

[MEASURED] B-RT machinery is sound: 100% pass (1,786 items checked, 0 failures) across the depth x clause-count grid (encoder content-hash 40e8c8ba...). Parse -> roll-up -> URN -> expand -> re-parse is an exact identity on the covered fragment, and D=8192 vectors are byte-identical across the round trip. The PARK is about corpus ENGAGEMENT, not correctness of the roll-up/expansion path — nothing is broken.

[MEASURED] OR grammar-extension decision number (a clean secondary deliverable): OR-forfeit is clause 0 / 0.0000% and NP 83-90 / ~0.0045-0.0050% of mass. The missing disjunction operator forfeits essentially nothing. Per design SS3.1 ('the decision is a number, not a taste'), this resolves AGAINST adding OR at v0 — an ALGORITHM_VERSION bump + X0 regen + Phase-X re-run is not licensed by <0.005% of forfeited mass.

[MEASURED] Dedupe / global-dictionary sizing: 911-1,145 unique URNs at dedupe ratios 6.6-8.0 (occurrences/unique). The invented-concept dictionary is small at this coverage; the promotion-to-named-concept opportunity (a coverage feedback into idea A) is real but tiny in absolute terms at the current instance.

[READ, idea A] B-E0 does NOT lower idea-A's priority. Idea A's estimand is cross-lingual value(c) = sum_l w_l sum_s f_l(s)(bpe_l(s)-1)*mhat(s,l) — token savings concentrated where BPE fertility is high (non-English), a DIFFERENT quantity from B-E0's English word-mass roll-up engagement, on a different corpus, via a different mechanism (minting NEW coverage vs rolling up EXISTING coverage; ideas explicitly decoupled, io-compression-ideas.md SS2.8). Parking idea B if anything RAISES the relative value of resolving idea A, because coverage growth is the lever idea B parks behind.
does_not_license: [ENVELOPE-BOUND] m0b-verbatim: MEASURED on TinyStories-valid ONLY, on ONE incomplete kernel-v0 + molecules-v0 instance (m0b coverage 0.3542 @ molecules-v0, NOT general coverage). The 0.4-0.6% numbers extrapolate to NO other corpus or kernel state; a number quoted outside this scope re-classifies as EXTRAPOLATION.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Engagement is engagement OF THIS conservative fail-closed v0 instrument (parser.mjs conventions), NOT a grammar ceiling and NOT the roll-up idea's asymptotic value. It bounds the idea at CURRENT coverage; it says nothing about engagement after coverage growth (that is the re-entry-gate measurement).

[EXTRAPOLATION, not a premise] 'A second (instruction/QA) corpus would show even lower engagement' is the expected DIRECTION (larger vocabulary => more unmapped-word abstention), mechanically grounded but of unmeasured magnitude. It is used only to explain why a 2nd corpus is not a rescue path; it is NOT the park's premise (the park stands on the favourable-corpus measurement alone). load_bearing:false; resolution_path = run the 2nd census corpus.

[BOUND] The PARK is not a permanent mechanism kill. K-B0 explicitly parks the idea behind coverage growth and/or the OR extension WITH THE CENSUS AS THE RE-ENTRY GATE. Re-run B-E0 after each kernel/mapper coverage-growth wave; the idea re-enters when achievable savings clears the (freeze-time) floor. This couples idea-B's fate to the coverage-growth track (define-op census / ingestion plan) and to idea A.

[NOT LICENSED] No consumption / accuracy claim of any kind. B-E1 (Tier 2-3, ~+$20-60) was NOT run and is NOT recommended now: it would measure a ~0.6%-engagement mechanism, coverage-dominated by construction, and cannot yield an informative compression-accuracy Pareto at this coverage. Authorizing B-E1 now would spend on a coverage ceiling.

[DESIGN-BOUND / BOUNDARY-STOP] Host-BPE token savings are UNMEASURED: no pinned host tokenizer on box, so B-E0 spans are word-token and character denominated, not host-BPE. The 0.4% is a word-mass number; the design SS1.5 accounting identity needs the pinned tokenizer for the true prefill-token savings. The floor comparison is done in word-mass as a proxy; the direction (~5x below) is robust to the currency but the exact multiple is tokenizer-dependent.

idea_a_a_e2_read.state: A-E2 is BLOCKED-ON-INPUTS — no result exists to interpret; this is a priority/authorization read only.
idea_a_a_e2_read.worth_the_fetch: [READ] Two-tier recommendation. (1) The HOST-TOKENIZER fetch (SmolLM2 tokenizer.json, the mandatory R1 tokenizer; small, deterministic, no compute) is HIGH-value and cheap and is DUAL-USE: it unblocks the host-BPE consumption currency for BOTH ideas (the a-e2-blocker 'consequence' field notes B-E0's word-mass-only limitation has the same root cause). Recommend authorizing it as the cheapest unblock. (2) The FREQUENCY-CORPUS fetch is the K-A2 gate and idea A's live-or-die number, so it is worth doing on the merits — BUT which lists (wordfreq-lineage / Leipzig / OSCAR / CC-100), which languages, the A-F1 cross-lingual alignment fork, and the pilot-language stipulation are DESIGN CHOICES NOT FROZEN. Per feasibility-push rules I do not improvise them: boundary-stop (see boundary_stops). B-E0 does not change this recommendation — it neither raises nor lowers idea A's fundamental priority, because it measures a different estimand.
idea_a_a_e2_read.b_e0_cross_caution_for_a: [READ] The one cross-cutting signal B-E0 sends idea A: the binding term in the shared English pipeline is mappability/vocabulary coverage (unmapped-word dominates abstention), and idea A's mhat term IS that mappability. So idea A's value curve will be mhat-discounted and K-A4 (mappability collapse: effective kappa_A under the floor) is a LIVE risk to price into A-E2 up front. This is weak, English-only evidence that mhat is thin on covered English; it says nothing about cross-lingual mhat and is a caution, not a kill.

epistemic_promotions.claim: at current coverage, compositional roll-up over real text achieves achievable corpus savings ~0.4% (< the K-B0 2% sanity floor); the binding constraint is vocabulary coverage, not the roll-up grammar

epistemic_promotions.scope: TinyStories-valid, kernel-v0+molecules-v0 instance, fail-closed v0 parser; extrapolates to no other corpus or kernel state

epistemic_promotions.claim: the missing OR operator forfeits negligible corpus mass (<0.005%) at v0

epistemic_promotions.scope: same corpus/instance; the forfeit number is corpus-indexed

epistemic_promotions.claim: the roll-up/expansion machinery is an exact round-trip identity (100%)
epistemic_promotions.scope: encoder content-hash 40e8c8ba..., the seeded generator + validity-preserving mutator grid

epistemic_promotions.claim: any statement about roll-up value after coverage growth, on other corpora, in host-BPE currency, or on any accuracy endpoint

forks_resolved.resolution: TRIGGERED-AS-INTERPRETATION (not a fired verdict — nothing frozen): achievable savings ~0.40-0.44% is ~5x below the 2% sanity default on the favourable corpus. Idea B PARKED behind coverage growth / OR extension; census is the re-entry gate. The two named caveats do not rescue (favourable corpus is best-case; permissiveness delta ~10% and off-target).
DEFER AT V0: OR-forfeit clause 0.0000% / NP <=0.005%. Not worth an ALGORITHM_VERSION bump at current coverage; revisit only if a coverage-grown re-run shows disjunction forfeiting real mass.
PASSED: 1,786/1,786, 0 failures. Machinery is exact; the park is about engagement, not correctness.

forks_resolved.fork: idea A vs idea B priority coupling
forks_resolved.resolution: DECOUPLED estimands (SS2.8); B-E0 does not lower idea A. Parking idea B raises the relative value of the coverage-growth track that idea A serves.

questions_opened.question: Negative-completion (NOT a rescue): run B-E0 on the stipulated 2nd corpus (instruction/QA) to complete the negative record the design planned (2 corpora). Expected direction is DOWN (larger vocabulary => more unmapped-word abstention); it strengthens the park, cannot flip it. Cheap and non-blocking.

RE-ENTRY GATE: re-run B-E0 after each kernel/mapper coverage-growth wave (haiku-tier mint, ingestion). Idea B re-enters when achievable savings clears the freeze-time floor. This is the standing coupling of idea B to the coverage-growth track.
Authorize the SmolLM2 host tokenizer.json fetch (mandatory R1 tokenizer; dual-use — unblocks host-BPE currency for BOTH A-E2 and a re-denominated B-E0). Cheapest unblock; no compute, no model call.

MAINTAINER/DESIGN sign-off needed before A-E2 can run: which frequency lists (wordfreq-lineage / Leipzig / OSCAR / CC-100, hash-pinned, fail-closed on provenance), which languages, the A-F1 cross-lingual alignment fork, and the pilot-language stipulation. Not improvised here (boundary-stop). Once signed, A-E2 is the K-A2 gate / idea A's live-or-die number.

When A-E2 is designed, price K-A4 (mappability collapse) up front: B-E0 shows vocabulary/mappability is the binding term on covered English; idea A's mhat term inherits that risk. Report m-hat-weighted engagement alongside membership, per K-A4.

_questions_note: est_cost_usd / est_agent_days are STIPULATED dry-plan estimates, never measurements. b-e0-second-corpus and a-e2-tokenizer-unblock are cheap and now-eligible; a-e2-frequency-corpus-signoff is a maintainer decision, not a Tier-0 run.

ledger_updates.statement: Compositional roll-up (idea B) achievable corpus savings is ~0.40-0.44% of word mass (engagement 0.59-0.64%), ~5x below the K-B0 2% sanity floor, on the favourable m0b corpus at the molecules-v0 instance; clause-AND engages ~0 (1 fragment / 405,523 sentences). Interpretive PARK (no frozen kill). Binding constraint = vocabulary coverage (unmapped-word 2.07M abstains), not the roll-up grammar.
ledger_updates.scope: TinyStories-valid 94e43181...; kernel-v0+molecules-v0 (m0b molecules-v0 rung); fail-closed v0 parser; word-mass (not host-BPE) currency

ledger_updates.statement: OR-forfeit is clause 0.0000% / NP <=0.005% of corpus mass — the missing disjunction operator forfeits negligible mass; defer OR at v0.
Roll-up/expansion round-trip (B-RT) is an exact identity: 1,786/1,786 items, 0 failures, across the depth x clause-count grid; D=8192 vectors byte-identical across the round trip.
ledger_updates.scope: encoder content-hash 40e8c8ba...; seeded generator + validity-preserving mutator

ledger_updates.statement: A-E2 (cross-lingual value curve) is blocked-on-inputs: pinned per-language frequency lists, host tokenizers, and non-English surface data are all absent locally; the fetch + source-pinning + pilot-language + A-F1 fork are unfrozen decisions.

boundary_stops.item: A-E2 frequency-corpus source-pinning + language selection + A-F1 alignment fork + pilot-language stipulation
boundary_stops.reason: Unfrozen DESIGN choices (which lists, which languages, which cross-lingual alignment data). Per feasibility-push rules I do not improvise; recommend maintainer/design sign-off (stub a-e2-frequency-corpus-signoff). My interpretive input: worth doing on the merits (K-A2 gate), and B-E0 does not lower its priority.

boundary_stops.item: Authorization of any external fetch (tokenizer or frequency lists)
boundary_stops.reason: Tier-0 constraint is $0/no-fetch; external download requires maintainer authorization. I RECOMMEND the SmolLM2 tokenizer fetch (cheap, dual-use) but cannot authorize it. The actual go/no-go is the maintainer's.

tree_impact: compositional-rollup (idea B) mini-tree: PARK route fires — mechanism_state=parked, re-entry gate = a coverage-grown B-E0 re-run clears the freeze-time floor. B-E1 (consumption, Tier 2-3) stays BLOCKED and is explicitly NOT recommended now (coverage-dominated). OR grammar extension: DEFERRED at v0 (OR-forfeit ~0). B-RT: PASSED, machinery banked. crosslingual-phrase-coverage (idea A): priority UNCHANGED by B-E0; A-E2 remains blocked-on-inputs — the tokenizer fetch is recommended as the cheapest dual-use unblock, the frequency-corpus decisions are a maintainer/design boundary-stop. No verdict object exists for any of these (pre-freeze gating censuses); this record is interpretation, not a fired kill. Coordinator action (not done here, out of scope): record the park + defer B-E1 spend; the interpretive-status tracker update is the coordinator's (I did not touch audit-status.jsonl).
